quests.name: Welcome to Thornwall
quests.description: Barthos wants you to get acquainted with the town by visiting the key establishments.

objectives.description: Visit the Iron Forge
Visit the General Store
Visit the Guard Tower

dialogue.offer: Welcome, traveler! You look like you just crawled out of the wilds. Let me point you around Thornwall before you get yourself killed. Visit the forge, the general store, and the guard tower -- you will need friends in all three before long.
dialogue.progress: Still finding your way? The town is not that big. Keep looking around.
dialogue.complete: Good, now you know where everything is. Thornwall is small, but it is the only safe place for leagues. You will want to remember that.

quests.name: First Blood
quests.description: Barthos suggests you prove you can handle yourself in a fight by slaying a few creatures near town.

objectives.description: Slay 3 creatures outside the safe zone

dialogue.offer: Now that you know your way around, let me give you some honest advice. The wilds beyond our walls are crawling with things that want to eat you. Go out there and put down three of them. If you come back alive, I will know you are worth talking to.
dialogue.progress: Still breathing? Good. Keep at it -- three kills, no fewer.
dialogue.complete: Ha! You have got some fight in you after all. Here, you have earned a drink on the house. Well, not on the house exactly -- here is some coin instead.

quests.name: Thin the Herd
quests.description: The Guard Captain needs the creature population around town kept under control.

objectives.description: Slay 5 creatures

dialogue.offer: The creatures beyond the walls have been breeding again. My guards can hold the perimeter, but we need someone to push them back. Kill five of them and report back to me.
dialogue.progress: The job is not done yet, soldier. Five kills. I do not accept half-measures.
dialogue.complete: Efficient work. The patrols will have an easier time tonight thanks to you. Take this -- standard bounty for pest control.

quests.name: The Western Menace
quests.description: Creatures have been spotted dangerously close to town from the west. Investigate and eliminate them.

objectives.description: Slay 3 creatures in the western wilderness

dialogue.offer: Our western scouts have not reported back in two days. Something is stirring out past the tree line, near the old stone pillars. I need you to head west and clear out whatever is lurking there. The coordinates are roughly 1500 paces west of here.
dialogue.progress: The western threat still stands. Do not come back until you have dealt with it.
dialogue.complete: The scouts finally reported in -- said they saw you carving through those things like a professional. Whatever was rallying them out west has been scattered. Well done.

quests.name: Northern Expedition
quests.description: Reports of dangerous creatures in the far north. The Guard Captain needs a capable fighter to investigate.

objectives.description: Slay 4 creatures in the northern reaches
Reach the Northern Ridge

dialogue.offer: There is something far to the north and east that has my veterans spooked. They talk about a ridge where the rocks jut up like broken teeth. I need someone who is not afraid of a long march and a hard fight. Clear the area and get me eyes on that ridge.
dialogue.progress: The north is a long walk. I hope you packed supplies.
dialogue.complete: You made it to the ridge and back. My veterans will stop their grumbling now. What you described up there... it concerns me. But that is a problem for another day. Take your pay.

quests.name: Into the Deep Wild
quests.description: The Guard Captain suspects something is organizing the creatures. Venture into the far reaches and eliminate the threat.

objectives.description: Slay 5 creatures in the deep eastern wilderness
Slay 3 creatures in the southern marshes

dialogue.offer: I have been piecing together the patrol reports, and I do not like the picture. The creatures are not just wandering -- they are converging on two points. One far to the east, another deep south in the marshes. I need you to hit both locations hard. This is the most dangerous mission I have given anyone. Are you ready?
dialogue.progress: Both locations need to be cleared. Do not leave the job half done.
dialogue.complete: Both nests cleared. You have done what a full patrol could not. Whatever is drawing those creatures together, you have set it back. Thornwall owes you a real debt. Take this -- you have earned every coin.

quests.name: Field Samples
quests.description: Elara needs someone to scout several locations for trade route viability.

objectives.description: Scout the southern tree line
Scout the eastern rock formation

dialogue.offer: I have been thinking about expanding my supply lines, but I need to know what is out there. Would you scout a couple of locations for me? I need to know if the southern tree line is passable for carts, and whether the eastern rock formations have anything worth mining. I will pay well for good information.
dialogue.progress: Have you scouted both locations yet? I need the full picture before I can plan my routes.
dialogue.complete: Excellent report. The southern route is promising, and those eastern rocks -- if there is ore in those formations, it could change everything for Thornwall. Here is your payment, and you have my gratitude.

quests.name: Rare Materials
quests.description: Elara has heard rumors of valuable resources in the far corners of the world.

objectives.description: Find the Crystal Cavern entrance in the far southwest
Locate the Ancient Quarry to the northeast
Clear 3 creatures guarding the routes

dialogue.offer: Your scouting work was excellent. Now I have something bigger. Old maps mention a crystal cavern far to the southwest, and a quarry to the northeast where the ancients cut stone for their cities. If either of those still exists, the trade possibilities are enormous. But those areas are deep in the wilds -- you will need to fight your way there.
dialogue.progress: Both sites need to be confirmed. I cannot invest in rumors alone.
dialogue.complete: They are real. Both of them, still intact after all these years. This changes everything, my friend. When the trade routes open, Thornwall will become a proper city. You will be remembered for this. Here -- a generous finder's fee.

quests.name: Supply Run
quests.description: Elara needs someone to check on a supply cache she left outside town.

objectives.description: Check the supply cache east of town
Clear any creatures near the cache

dialogue.offer: I left a supply cache east of town near a large boulder. I need someone to check on it -- last time I sent the Farmer he came running back screaming about monsters. Clear anything nasty away from the area and make sure the cache is still intact.
dialogue.progress: Is the cache still there? Please tell me nothing ate my inventory.
dialogue.complete: Cache is intact and the area is clear? Perfect. Those supplies will keep my shelves stocked for another week. Thank you -- take a little something for your trouble.

quests.name: Arms and Armor
quests.description: Grimhammer needs field-tested materials to improve his smithing.

objectives.description: Collect hides from 5 slain creatures
Gather stone samples from the mossy deposit south of town

dialogue.offer: Bah! The materials in this backwater are barely fit for horseshoes. I need creature hides for leather working and stone samples from a deposit I spotted south of here -- mossy rocks, hard to miss. Bring me both and I will make it worth your while. And do not bring me anything chewed on.
dialogue.progress: I need those hides AND the stone samples. One without the other is useless to me.
dialogue.complete: Now THIS is something I can work with. The hide quality is decent, and this stone... it has a good grain to it. Proper smithing requires proper materials. You have done well. Here is your pay.

quests.name: The Old Anvil
quests.description: Grimhammer has heard of an ancient forge site in the wilderness and wants it investigated.

objectives.description: Find the ruins of the ancient forge among the stone spires
Clear 4 creatures nesting in the ruins

dialogue.offer: Listen, I have been studying the markings on that stone you brought me. It was cut, not broken. Someone was forging out there long before Thornwall existed. The old maps show stone spires to the southwest -- I think there was a forge there once. Find it for me. And whatever moved in since the smiths left, put it down.
dialogue.progress: Have you found the old forge yet? Those stone spires should be hard to miss.
dialogue.complete: You found it! And the foundation stones are still intact? Incredible. The ancients knew how to build. The techniques etched into those stones... I have never seen anything like it. This will take my craft to a new level. Worth every coin.

quests.name: Masterwork Materials
quests.description: Grimhammer needs rare materials from dangerous locations to attempt a masterwork creation.

objectives.description: Collect ore from the iron vein in the western rock clusters
Slay 8 creatures for their hardened bone
Collect water from the Ember Pool in the far northwest

dialogue.offer: The techniques from the old forge -- I have been studying them day and night. I can make something extraordinary, but I need extraordinary materials. Raw iron from the western veins. Hardened bone from eight creatures -- the older ones, the tougher ones. And water from a thermal pool the ancients called the Ember Pool, far to the northwest. This will be dangerous. But the blade I forge from these materials will be legendary.
dialogue.progress: I need all three components. Iron, bone, and Ember Pool water. No shortcuts in masterwork smithing.
dialogue.complete: Everything, in perfect condition. You are either very skilled or very lucky, and I suspect it is the former. Stand back -- when I begin this forging, the hammer strikes will ring for a day and a night. You have given me the chance to create something that will outlast us both. Here is your reward, and my respect.

quests.name: Whispers in Stone
quests.description: The Wanderer speaks of strange markings on rocks that predate all known settlements.

objectives.description: Examine the mossy rocks east of town
Examine the stone spire to the west

dialogue.offer: You have the look of someone who asks questions. Good -- I have too many questions and not enough answers. There are stones in the wilds with markings on them. Not natural cracks, mind you -- deliberate carvings. Someone was here before any of us. I have seen two such stones but I am too old and too slow to study them properly. Will you go look?
dialogue.progress: The stones are not going anywhere, but my patience is. Have you examined them both?
dialogue.complete: You saw them too, then. The spiral pattern, the counting marks... these are not decorative. Someone was keeping records -- or leaving warnings. I need to think on this. Here, take this for your time. I suspect this is only the beginning.

quests.name: The Old Roads
quests.description: The Wanderer believes the marker stones trace a path to something important.

objectives.description: Find the third marker stone in the far west
Find the fourth marker stone to the south
Defeat 3 creatures guarding the marker sites

dialogue.offer: I have been mapping the two stones you found, and the spiral patterns -- they point directions. Like a road made of landmarks. I believe there are at least two more stones further out. One should be far to the west, another to the south. If I am right, these markers trace a path to something the ancients wanted found. Or wanted hidden.
dialogue.progress: The path has more stones. Keep searching -- this is too important to abandon.
dialogue.complete: Four stones, and the pattern is unmistakable now. They form a diamond shape, and at the center... nothing. Yet. But the counting marks increase on each stone. They are counting toward something. Or counting down. I need to study my maps further. This discovery is remarkable -- take this reward and know that you have uncovered something that may change what we know about this land.

quests.name: Heart of the Wilds
quests.description: The Wanderer has calculated the center point of the ancient markers. Something waits there.

objectives.description: Reach the convergence point of the ancient markers
Defeat the creatures drawn to the convergence

dialogue.offer: I have done it. Four markers, four directions, and they all point to one place -- a convergence point west-southwest of town. The counting marks on the stones are definitely a countdown. Whatever the ancients left there, they wanted someone to find it. They also wanted to make sure only someone determined enough would reach it. The wilds around the convergence will be thick with creatures. Are you determined enough?
dialogue.progress: The convergence awaits. I know it is dangerous, but we have come too far to turn back now.
dialogue.complete: You found it. The foundation stones, the circle of carved pillars... this was a meeting place. Maybe a place of power. The ancients gathered here, possibly from settlements we have not yet found. This means Thornwall is not alone -- or at least, it was not always alone. You have rewritten history today, my friend. I do not have enough gold to truly repay you, but take what I have. The knowledge is the real treasure.

quests.name: Patrol Duty
quests.description: Help the Guard Captain maintain the town perimeter by eliminating nearby threats.

objectives.description: Slay 8 creatures

dialogue.offer: I am short on guards today and the perimeter needs sweeping. Eight creatures, no particular direction -- just make the area around Thornwall safer. Standard patrol bounty applies.
dialogue.progress: Eight kills, not seven, not six. I keep count, and so should you.
dialogue.complete: Full patrol complete. The perimeter is holding thanks to you. Same time tomorrow if you are interested -- there is always more work.

quests.name: The Farmer's Worry
quests.description: The Farmer is concerned about creatures threatening the fields outside town.

objectives.description: Slay 3 creatures near the farmlands

dialogue.offer: I have been trying to work the fields south of the walls, but those blasted creatures keep getting closer. I cannot plant with one hand on a pitchfork. Could you clear a few of them out? I am not asking for miracles -- just three of the beasts gone so I can get a crop in the ground.
dialogue.progress: Are they gone yet? I have seeds that need planting before the season turns.
dialogue.complete: Bless you, friend. I can finally work without looking over my shoulder every ten seconds. It is not much, but take these coins -- a farmer always pays his debts.

quests.name: Lost and Found
quests.description: The Peasant has lost a family heirloom somewhere outside the town walls.

objectives.description: Search the old campsite southeast of town

dialogue.offer: Please, I need help. My grandmother's pendant -- I dropped it when I fled from a creature last week. I was camped southeast of town, not too far out. I would go myself but... well, you have seen what is out there. Could you search the campsite for me?
dialogue.progress: Did you find the campsite? It should be southeast of the walls, near some scattered trees.
dialogue.complete: You found it! Oh, thank the stars. My grandmother would have haunted me if I had lost this for good. I do not have much, but please take what I can offer. You are a good soul.

quests.name: The Distant Spires
quests.description: The Wanderer wants to know what lies at the very edges of the known world.

objectives.description: Explore the far northwestern corner of the world
Explore the far southeastern corner of the world

dialogue.offer: I have wandered far, but never to the very edges. The northwestern wastes and the southeastern reaches -- those are places even I have avoided. But someone should know what is out there. Will you be the first to map the extremes? It is a long journey with no guarantee of safety, but think of the stories you will tell.
dialogue.progress: Both corners of the world, remember. I want the full picture.
dialogue.complete: Both edges of the world, seen by living eyes and returned to tell the tale. Remarkable. What you describe -- the terrain, the silence, the sense of something vast and sleeping -- it matches the old legends perfectly. The world is larger and stranger than any of us knew. Your name belongs on the maps now.
